info.title: Generate generic Spoon rule for Jedis API breaking change

parts.text: "Project @JRedisGraph/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @JRedisGraph/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/jedis-4.3.2-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-jedis-4.3.2/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @JRedisGraph/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Modifying code efficiently**

I need to modify the code in the spoon-base-template and inspect the repository for any issues. It looks like using commentary updates and a to-do list will help since the code is complex. I ought to identify compile errors in JRedisGraph and use the right tools. I shouldn't use bash for reading files but can use it for compilation. Maybe considering a task agent could help, but I think I’ll create a detailed to-do list first.
I’m locating the affected project, the Spoon template, and the dependency docs first so I can derive the API break generically before writing the transformation.

todos.content: Inspect JRedisGraph compilation failures and identify the breaking Jedis API change
Derive a generic call-site transformation from old API pattern to new API pattern
Compile and validate the Spoon transformation
Run transformation on JRedisGraph and verify project builds/tests
Check the rule for project-specific identifiers and generalizability

parts.text:
**Identifying compilation errors**

I need to identify the compilation errors, which might be due to the removal of certain RedisGraphQueryUtil methods. It makes sense to inspect the source in JRedisGraph for any graph command invocations. I could use grep patterns to see if the source utilizes specific imports, but my initial attempt at grep with a literal pattern returned no files, possibly due to regex issues. So, I'll need to check the files directly and read through the likely API files.
I’ve narrowed the break to Jedis graph APIs. I need the exact JRedisGraph call sites now so I can map old overloads to the new `RedisGraphQueryUtil.prepareQuery`/graph command split in a generic way.

parts.text:
**Examining dependency issues**

I need to focus on addressing the breaking changes due to an outdated package that’s been moved or removed. There are a lot of compilation errors related to source incompatibility with Jedis 4.3.2, not just with the graph API. The user wants me to identify these compilation errors in JRedisGraph and figure out which API changed in the dependencies. It looks like the old Jedis transaction and pipeline APIs have changed. I’ll need to inspect the specific altered API in Jedis 4.3.2 and possibly adjust our transformation for any affected projects.

The build failure is broader than graph commands: Jedis 4.3.2 moved several core types out of `redis.clients.jedis.commands` and changed pipeline/transaction base APIs. I’m identifying the concrete replacement types from the new API so the rule can rewrite those structurally, not just in this repo.

parts.text:
**Considering Jedis transformation**

I need to implement a generic transformation that updates imports, constructors, and query methods. There's a possibility that the old API has specific failures. The user is looking for a reusable way to update dependencies, likely due to changes in Jedis 4.3.2 where `Client` switched to `Connection`. I should inspect all compilation errors related to imports, especially with `redis.clients.jedis.Client` missing. It seems I need to replace `Client` with `Connection` in constructor signatures and check if the Transaction class still exists.

**Assessing changes in Jedis API**

I see that the `Client` type was removed, and now constructors require `Connection`. A good approach would be to replace `redis.clients.jedis.Client` with `redis.clients.jedis.Connection` in imports and constructor parameters. I should check for other types that may have moved from `redis.clients.jedis.commands.*` into `redis.clients.jedis.*`. The errors in `RedisGraphPipeline.java` and `RedisGraphTransaction.java` likely stem from older user-defined interfaces. These may need to be updated to extend newer interfaces instead. I should inspect the API documentation for clarity on these changes.

**Inspecting source files for changes**

It seems the user is asking for a generic transformation rule related to a breaking change, specifically about package relocation and modified constructors or methods. To address this, I should take a closer look at the old source in the root interfaces to understand the actual compilation errors. Reading through those files will help clarify the necessary changes to ensure everything aligns correctly with the updated structure. This will guide me in providing an effective transformation solution.

I’ve confirmed this is a mixed break: package relocations plus constructor/base-class API changes. I’m reading the remaining affected interfaces to define a single structural rewrite rule that strips obsolete Jedis base interfaces and updates direct Jedis types.

1: package com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.api;
2: 
3: import java.util.List;
4: 
5: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.RedisGraphContext;
6: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.ResultSet;
7: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.exceptions.JRedisGraphException;
8: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.Utils;

10: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.resultset.ResultSetImpl;
11:

14: import redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisDataException;
15: import redis.clients.jedis.util.SafeEncoder;
16: 
17: /**
18:  * An implementation of RedisGraphContext. Allows sending RedisGraph and some Redis commands,
19:  * within a specific connection context
20:  */
21: public class ContextedRedisGraph extends AbstractRedisGraph implements RedisGraphContext, RedisGraphCacheHolder {
22: 
23:     private final Jedis connectionContext;
24:     private RedisGraphCaches caches;
25: 
26:     /**
27:      * Generates a new instance with a specific Jedis connection
28:      * @param connectionContext
29:      */
30:     public ContextedRedisGraph(Jedis connectionContext) {
31:         this.connectionContext = connectionContext;
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Overrides the abstract method. Return the instance only connection
36:      * @return
37:      */
38:     @Override
39:     protected Jedis getConnection() {
40:         return this.connectionContext;
41:     }
42: 
43:     /**
44:      * Sends the query over the instance only connection
45:      * @param graphId graph to be queried
46:      * @param preparedQuery prepared query
47:      * @return Result set with the query answer
48:      */
49:     @Override
50:     protected ResultSet sendQuery(String graphId, String preparedQuery) {
51:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
52:         try {
53:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
54:             List<Object> rawResponse = (List<Object>) conn.sendCommand(RedisGraphCommand.QUERY, graphId, preparedQuery, Utils.COMPACT_STRING);
55:             return new ResultSetImpl(rawResponse, this, caches.getGraphCache(graphId));
56:         } catch (JRedisGraphException rt) {
57:             throw rt;
58:         } catch (JedisDataException j) {

18: 
19:     private static final CharSequenceTranslator ESCAPE_CHYPER;

21:         final Map<CharSequence, CharSequence> escapeJavaMap = new HashMap<>();

23:         escapeJavaMap.put("\"", "\\\"");

29:     /**

32:      * @return the input string surrounded with quotation marks, if needed
33:      */
34:     private static String quoteString(String str){
35:         StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(str.length()+2);
36:         sb.append('"');
37:         sb.append(str.replace("\"","\\\""));
38:         sb.append('"');
39:         return sb.toString();

56:                 }

70:         StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("CYPHER ");

72:             String key = entry.getKey();

74:             sb.append(key).append('=');

76:             sb.append(' ');

79:         return sb.toString();

83:         StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder().append('[');

85:         sb.append(']');
86:         return sb.toString();
87:     }
88: 
89:     private static String valueToString(Object value) {
90:         if(value == null) return "null";
91: 
92:         if(value instanceof String){
93:             return quoteString((String) value);
94:         }
95:         if(value instanceof Character){
96:             return quoteString(((Character)value).toString());
97:         }
98:

100:             return arrayToString((Object[]) value);
101: 
102:         }

public class Launcher 103: extends Object 104: implements SpoonAPI
105:
This class implements an integrated command-line launcher for processing 106: programs at compile-time using the JDT-based builder (Eclipse). It takes 107: arguments that allow building, processing, printing, and compiling Java 108: programs. Launch with no arguments (see main(String[])) for detailed 109: usage.

parts.text:
**Considering project changes**

In any project affected by the same break, it looks like I need to change pipeline subclass constructors and transaction subclasses. Additionally, I should remove specific imports from our interfaces. I want to ensure structural matching of classes extending `Pipeline` and `Transaction`. This involves replacing constructor bodies or modifying superclass constructor arguments for broader applicability. It's essential to be systematic about these adjustments to maintain project integrity.

**Inspecting code transformations**

I’m inspecting the current code and planning some generic transformations. First, I'll replace `redis.clients.jedis.Client` with `redis.clients.jedis.Connection`. Then, I need to check for any imports related to removed `redis.clients.jedis.commands.*` marker interfaces and remove those from both imports and type references. For class constructors extending Pipeline, I’ll change `super.setClient(client);` to `super(client);` to ensure proper functionality.

**Evaluating transaction behavior for tests**

I think transaction behavior might not get exercised in tests, which could lead to issues. Fixing these errors and inspections seems crucial. It’s likely that I’d want to implement patches thoroughly throughout the code. I also need to check for any additional compile errors that could pop up, possibly linked to `Main.java` or issues in the source tree. For validation, I’ll have to ensure the transformation refers to a specific input directory, while crafting `Main.java` to handle input appropriately.

**Refining manual scan process**

I’m thinking of utilizing a simpler manual scan with `getModel().getElements` and loops. I’ll use a factory to generate connection references and so forth. When it comes to type references, I’ve got a plan: if a reference's qualified name equals `redis.clients.jedis.Client`, I’ll set it to Connection. Also, if any reference is in the obsolete set, I’ll remove it if it appears in extends, implements, or imports. This approach should help streamline the process!

parts.text:
**Evaluating Spoon transformation**

I need to edit Main.java to create a generic Spoon transformer. The user wants to save the transformation in a specific file, so I’ll likely have to use the Spoon launcher to explore the source directory and perform the transformation. I should understand the template's compile needs and the package layout. 

It seems I need to check if the old package names still exist since the compile isn’t finding some interfaces. I might need to remove old imports and possibly extend the new interfaces instead.

**Considering dependency updates**

It looks like I’m dealing with a generic transformation for interfaces that extend removed marker interfaces. The user mentioned breaking dependencies, but the actual build errors are more extensive. I think the transformation should focus on fixing imports and superclass constructor signatures. 

I need to replace instances of `Client` with `Connection` in constructors and any calls to `getClient()` should switch to `getConnection()`. Also, I need to adjust the `Pipeline` constructor accordingly. It seems like they might want a broader transformation rule for upgrading from Jedis 3 to 4.3.2 across different projects.

**Updating constructor parameters**

In the subclass, I need to ensure the constructor that uses `Client client` passes a `Connection` to `super(client)`. The same applies to `Transaction` extending its parent class; the constructor should still accept a `Connection`. 

So, the rule here is to update constructor parameter types from `Client` to `Connection`, while also checking calls to `getClient()`. There are also compile errors due to outdated package imports for marker interfaces, so I’ll need to remove those from the interfaces, particularly in `RedisGraphPipeline` and `RedisGraphTransaction`.

**Analyzing code compilation error**

I see that there's a compilation error related to the `execGetResponse()` method in `RedisGraphTransaction`. It looks like the class is implementing an interface but isn't providing the required method. This might be because the interface originally didn't include that method, but now it does. It seems like this could be an issue with outdated code. The user wants me to fix the compilation and tests, so I should focus on resolving dependency-related errors to get it to compile correctly.

**Exploring interface implementation issues**

I'm analyzing the missing `execGetResponse` method in the class, which should lead to a compilation error. It seems that if the class implements an interface that originally extended old Jedis interfaces, it might not need to implement the method anymore. However, since the interface still declares it, the compilation issue has existed before. It looks like the class must now implement the method manually or remove it from the project interface, since the old dependencies are no longer available. This is getting complex!

**Examining compile issues and imports**

Maybe the user is looking for a specific breakdown related to `graphQuery`, but the compile errors indicate missing imports from old Jedi pipeline interfaces due to a package move. It seems like even after removing those interfaces, the project might still not compile fully because of missing methods. Should I run tests? To compile successfully, I might only need to add `execGetResponse` for the relevant dependency. The root interface extends the old pipeline, which contained methods like `execGetResponse`, `syncAndReturnAll`, and `waitReplicas`, all crucial for resolving this.

**Searching for method details**

I’m considering if the `GetResponse` method exists. I noticed in some Javadoc snippets that `TransactionBase` might have an `exec()` method, but I didn’t see `execGetResponse` when I searched through my files. I think the best way to determine if it’s there would be to search the Javadoc more thoroughly. Let’s see if I can find what I need by diving deeper into those documents.

The remaining break is partly structural: the project’s own pipeline/transaction interfaces were coupled to Jedis internals that no longer exist. I’m checking whether those methods are still present under a different Jedis superclass; if they are, I can keep the rule generic by rewriting call signatures and imported supertypes rather than project names.

**Implementing transformation rules**

I need to implement a transformation rule since the user wants a generic, reusable rule for any Maven project. I think it might require creating a Spoon processor that updates imports and method invocations. I need to identify the exact breaking API in the dependency since compilation errors are happening due to changed import paths. It looks like, in Jedis 4.3.2, those interfaces might be moved to a different package, so I need to inspect the Javadocs for the correct interfaces.

I’ve confirmed the old Jedis pipeline marker interfaces are gone from the 4.3.2 API surface. I’m locating their new homes now so I can encode a generic import/supertype rewrite instead of a JRedisGraph-specific patch.

1: package com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.api;
2: 
3: import java.util.List;
4: 
5: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.RedisGraphContext;
6: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.ResultSet;
7: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.exceptions.JRedisGraphException;
8: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.Utils;
9: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.graph_cache.RedisGraphCaches;
10: import com.redislabs.redisgraph.impl.resultset.ResultSetImpl;
11: 
12: import redis.clients.jedis.Client;
13: import redis.clients.jedis.Jedis;
14: import redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisDataException;
15: import redis.clients.jedis.util.SafeEncoder;
16: 
17: /**
18:  * An implementation of RedisGraphContext. Allows sending RedisGraph and some Redis commands,
19:  * within a specific connection context
20:  */
21: public class ContextedRedisGraph extends AbstractRedisGraph implements RedisGraphContext, RedisGraphCacheHolder {
22: 
23:     private final Jedis connectionContext;
24:     private RedisGraphCaches caches;
25: 
26:     /**
27:      * Generates a new instance with a specific Jedis connection
28:      * @param connectionContext
29:      */
30:     public ContextedRedisGraph(Jedis connectionContext) {
31:         this.connectionContext = connectionContext;
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Overrides the abstract method. Return the instance only connection
36:      * @return
37:      */
38:     @Override
39:     protected Jedis getConnection() {
40:         return this.connectionContext;
41:     }
42: 
43:     /**
44:      * Sends the query over the instance only connection
45:      * @param graphId graph to be queried
46:      * @param preparedQuery prepared query
47:      * @return Result set with the query answer
48:      */
49:     @Override
50:     protected ResultSet sendQuery(String graphId, String preparedQuery) {
51:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
52:         try {
53:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
54:             List<Object> rawResponse = (List<Object>) conn.sendCommand(RedisGraphCommand.QUERY, graphId, preparedQuery, Utils.COMPACT_STRING);
55:             return new ResultSetImpl(rawResponse, this, caches.getGraphCache(graphId));
56:         } catch (JRedisGraphException rt) {
57:             throw rt;
58:         } catch (JedisDataException j) {
59:             throw new JRedisGraphException(j);
60:         }
61:     }
62: 
63:     /**
64:      * Sends the read-only query over the instance only connection
65:      * @param graphId graph to be queried
66:      * @param preparedQuery prepared query
67:      * @return Result set with the query answer
68:      */
69:     @Override
70:     protected ResultSet sendReadOnlyQuery(String graphId, String preparedQuery) {
71:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
72:         try {
73:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
74:             List<Object> rawResponse = (List<Object>) conn.sendCommand(RedisGraphCommand.RO_QUERY, graphId, preparedQuery, Utils.COMPACT_STRING);
75:             return new ResultSetImpl(rawResponse, this, caches.getGraphCache(graphId));
76:         } catch (JRedisGraphException ge) {
77:             throw ge;
78:         } catch (JedisDataException de) {
79:             throw new JRedisGraphException(de);
80:         }
81:     }
82: 
83:     /**
84:      * Sends the query over the instance only connection
85:      * @param graphId graph to be queried
86:      * @param timeout
87:      * @param preparedQuery prepared query
88:      * @return Result set with the query answer
89:      */
90:     @Override
91:     protected ResultSet sendQuery(String graphId, String preparedQuery, long timeout) {
92:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
93:         try {
94:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
95:             List<Object> rawResponse = (List<Object>) conn.sendBlockingCommand(RedisGraphCommand.QUERY,
96:                     graphId, preparedQuery, Utils.COMPACT_STRING, Utils.TIMEOUT_STRING, Long.toString(timeout));
97:             return new ResultSetImpl(rawResponse, this, caches.getGraphCache(graphId));
98:         } catch (JRedisGraphException rt) {
99:             throw rt;
100:         } catch (JedisDataException j) {
101:             throw new JRedisGraphException(j);
102:         }
103:     }
104: 
105:     /**
106:      * Sends the read-only query over the instance only connection
107:      * @param graphId graph to be queried
108:      * @param timeout
109:      * @param preparedQuery prepared query
110:      * @return Result set with the query answer
111:      */
112:     @Override
113:     protected ResultSet sendReadOnlyQuery(String graphId, String preparedQuery, long timeout) {
114:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
115:         try {
116:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
117:             List<Object> rawResponse = (List<Object>) conn.sendBlockingCommand(RedisGraphCommand.RO_QUERY,
118:                     graphId, preparedQuery, Utils.COMPACT_STRING, Utils.TIMEOUT_STRING, Long.toString(timeout));
119:             return new ResultSetImpl(rawResponse, this, caches.getGraphCache(graphId));
120:         } catch (JRedisGraphException ge) {
121:             throw ge;
122:         } catch (JedisDataException de) {
123:             throw new JRedisGraphException(de);
124:         }
125:     }
126: 
127:     /**
128:      * @return Returns the instance Jedis connection.
129:      */
130:     @Override
131:     public Jedis getConnectionContext() {
132:         return this.connectionContext;
133:     }
134: 
135:     /**
136:      * Creates a new RedisGraphTransaction transactional object
137:      * @return new RedisGraphTransaction
138:      */
139:     @Override
140:     public RedisGraphTransaction multi() {
141:         Jedis jedis = getConnection();
142:         Client client = jedis.getClient();
143:         client.multi();
144:         client.getOne();
145:         RedisGraphTransaction transaction = new RedisGraphTransaction(client, this);
146:         transaction.setRedisGraphCaches(caches);
147:         return transaction;
148:     }
149: 
150:     /**
151:      * Creates a new RedisGraphPipeline pipeline object
152:      * @return new RedisGraphPipeline
153:      */
154:     @Override
155:     public RedisGraphPipeline pipelined() {
156:         Jedis jedis = getConnection();
157:         Client client = jedis.getClient();
158:         RedisGraphPipeline pipeline = new RedisGraphPipeline(client, this);
159:         pipeline.setRedisGraphCaches(caches);
160:         return pipeline;
161:     }
162: 
163:     /**
164:      * Perfrom watch over given Redis keys
165:      * @param keys
166:      * @return "OK"
167:      */
168:     @Override
169:     public String watch(String... keys) {
170:         return this.getConnection().watch(keys);
171:     }
172: 
173:     /**
174:      * Removes watch from all keys
175:      * @return
176:      */
177:     @Override
178:     public String unwatch() {
179:         return this.getConnection().unwatch();
180:     }
181: 
182:     /**
183:      * Deletes the entire graph
184:      * @param graphId graph to delete
185:      * @return delete running time statistics
186:      */
187:     @Override
188:     public String deleteGraph(String graphId) {
189:         Jedis conn = getConnection();
190:         Object response;
191:         try {
192:             response = conn.sendCommand(RedisGraphCommand.DELETE, graphId);
193:         } catch (Exception e) {
194:             conn.close();
195:             throw e;
196:         }
197:         //clear local state
198:         caches.removeGraphCache(graphId);
199:         return SafeEncoder.encode((byte[]) response);
200:     }
201: 
202:     /**
203:      * closes the Jedis connection
204:      */
205:     @Override
206:     public void close() {
207:         this.connectionContext.close();
208: 
209:     }
210: 
211:     @Override
212:     public void setRedisGraphCaches(RedisGraphCaches caches) {
213:         this.caches = caches;
214:     }
215: 
216: }

(End of file - total 216 lines)
</content>
